description_long: This is a large ceiling-mounted electric fan with five elongated, wooden-finished blades radiating from a central metallic hub. The central hub is circular and metallic, likely made of brass or a similar alloy, and is suspended from the ceiling by a straight, narrow rod. The blades are flat, slightly tapered, and have a rich brown wood grain appearance, giving the fan a classic and elegant look. There is no visible blade guard, leaving the blades exposed for maximum airflow. The fan's design is minimalistic, with no visible control panel or power cord, suggesting it is operated by a wall switch or remote. The overall construction appears sturdy, suitable for large indoor spaces such as living rooms, lobbies, or covered patios.
description: A large, ceiling-mounted electric fan with five wooden-finished blades and a metallic central hub, featuring an open, classic design with no visible controls or blade guard.
description_view_0: The fan is seen from below, showing all five blades radiating symmetrically from the central hub.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side profile of the blades and the central hub, with the suspension rod clearly visible.
description_view_2: The fan is viewed at an angle, emphasizing the depth and curvature of the blades as well as the metallic hub.
description_view_3: A rotated perspective showing the underside of one blade more prominently, with the central hub and suspension rod still visible.
attribute_blade_guard_design: There is no blade guard; the blades are fully exposed, emphasizing an open and unobstructed design.
